title: EDHOC signing key not zeroized in EdhocInitiator/EdhocResponder
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 177-193, 599-614

The EdhocInitiator and EdhocResponder structs contain SigningKey (Ed25519 private key):
```rust
pub struct EdhocInitiator {
    /// Our Ed25519 signing key.
    signing_key: SigningKey,
    ...
}
```

The state structs (InitiatorState, ResponderState) derive Zeroize/ZeroizeOnDrop, but the outer structs (EdhocInitiator, EdhocResponder) do NOT.

The SigningKey from ed25519-dalek does implement Zeroize, but without #[zeroize(drop)] on the outer struct, dropping an EdhocInitiator/EdhocResponder won't zeroize the signing_key.

Impact: Ed25519 signing key may remain in memory after EDHOC completion.

Fix: Add #[derive(Zeroize)] and #[zeroize(drop)] to EdhocInitiator and EdhocResponder:
```rust
#[derive(Zeroize)]
#[zeroize(drop)]
pub struct EdhocInitiator {
    signing_key: SigningKey,
    ...
}
```
